payload.title: Decouple the bug-catching lints from the named-arg dialect (run safety lints without R0042)
payload.description: R0042 (no-positional-args) was 83% of the reviewer's findings and is unsatisfiable in stock Rust — 'fixing' it needs named-arg syntax rustc rejects. The real value is R0001 (unwrap), R0003 (as-cast), R0014 (bare-index), R0018 (dropped-error-context). Today R0042 is baked into the always-on ALL set (crates/trust-lints/src/rules.rs:221-242); a `lint_with()` subset API exists (crates/trust-lints/src/runner.rs:47-50) but no CLI flag exposes it. Need: a way to run a chosen rule subset (e.g. `trust check --rules safety` or `--allow R0042`) so Trust works as a plain-Rust advisory linter surfacing the ~22 genuine findings with zero dialect. THE change that unblocks the reviewer's realistic use of 0.1.0. Pairs with the config-file ticket.
